frozen_claim: Stage39-C safe_structured_v2, audited by Stage40-A, is approved as an explicit opt-in final composer under the evaluated profiles, while remaining disabled by default.

stage_summaries.stage33_f_f2: Establishes a structured coverage shadow owner; strong external Stage31 coverage diagnostics; final composer application still DENY at this point.
stage_summaries.stage34_c: Held-out generalization probe; support-oriented subset entailments generalize strongly as a shadow owner; reverse overclaim handling remains mixed; final composer still not enabled.
stage_summaries.stage35_a: Adversarial stress test reveals the Stage33-F shadow owner alone is unsafe on adversarial scope/exception cases (scope_safety=unsafe, reverse_handling=unsafe); motivated Stage36 blockers.
stage_summaries.stage36_a: Support safety blockers repair unsafe SUPPORT leakage (overclaim, exception, location-scope all driven to 0) without reducing existing SUPPORT recovery; scope_safety becomes safe; remaining issue is weak positive SUPPORT recovery.
stage_summaries.stage37_a: Safe support recovery adds additional SUPPORT recovery on top of Stage36 blockers with zero new unsafe leakage; support_shadow_support rises from 91 to 153; still shadow-only, final composer DENY.
stage_summaries.stage38_a: Integrated regression audit validates Stage33-F+Stage36+Stage37 as safe shadow diagnostic owner: zero dev prediction mismatches, adversarial safety and recovery checks pass. Final composer still requires explicit opt-in validation.
stage_summaries.stage39_a: support_only final composer is safe (zero unsafe SUPPORT counters) but too weak: Stage34 macro-F1 only reaches 0.6063, Stage35 only 0.5789.
stage_summaries.stage39_b: safe_structured final composer is a strong improvement (Stage34 macro-F1 0.8431, Stage35 macro-F1 0.7202) but Stage34 still misses some high-precision REFUTE coverage.
stage_summaries.stage39_c: safe_structured_v2 final composer converts the validated shadow owner into an explicit opt-in final composer, reaching Stage34 macro-F1 0.9703 and Stage35 macro-F1 0.7202 with zero introduced unsafe errors; still off by default; residual errors are inherited from the original model, not introduced by Stage39-C.
stage_summaries.stage40_a: Integrated final composer regression audit confirms Stage39-C safe_structured_v2 is safe as an explicit opt-in final-prediction replacement across dev, Stage34, and Stage35, with all aggregate introduced-unsafe counters at zero; composer must remain off by default; further external/naturalistic validation required.

final_composer_comparison.final_status: strong improvement, Stage34 still misses some high-precision REFUTE coverage

allowed_claims: The structured owner improves held-out synthetic coverage entailment diagnostics.
Stage36/37 separate safety blocking from safe support recovery.
Stage39-C can be used as an explicit opt-in final-prediction replacement under the evaluated profiles.
Stage40-A confirms no Stage39-C-introduced unsafe SUPPORT / REFUTE-to-SUPPORT / SUPPORT-to-REFUTE errors across dev/Stage34/Stage35.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim broad naturalistic NLI robustness.
Do not claim the composer should be enabled by default.
Do not claim learned neural understanding of coverage semantics.
Do not claim Stage34/35 are independent real-world benchmarks.
Do not claim zero total model errors, only zero introduced unsafe errors by the composer.
limitations: Stage39-C is rule/composer-mediated, not proof of neural semantic generalization.
Default-off behavior is policy-confirmed, not from an explicit default-off prediction diff inside Stage40.

default_off_policy.description: The Stage39-C safe_structured_v2 final composer requires explicit opt-in activation and ships disabled by default. Stage40-A's default_off_policy_ok pass flag confirms this policy remains in place; it is a policy confirmation, not an explicit default-off prediction diff performed inside Stage40.

leakage_policy.description: This document is reporting-only. It must not be used for training, calibration, threshold selection, checkpoint selection, or loss design. Stage34/35 probes must remain diagnostic/evaluation artifacts.

recommendation: Stage39-C safe_structured_v2 is safe as an explicit opt-in final-prediction replacement under evaluated profiles and must remain off by default. Probes/reports must not be used for training, calibration, threshold selection, checkpoint selection, or loss design. Further external/naturalistic validation is required before claiming broad production robustness.
